source_title: Projectile Motion and Time of Flight
subject: physics

sections.heading: Time of Flight
sections.text: Projectile motion can be split into horizontal and vertical motion. For a projectile launched from the ground and landing at the same height, the total time of flight depends on the vertical component of the initial velocity. The shortcut formula is T = 2u sin(theta) / g, where u is the launch speed, theta is the launch angle above the horizontal, and g is gravitational acceleration. This formula comes from vertical motion: the projectile rises until vertical velocity becomes zero, then falls back to the launch height. For theta = 30 degrees, sin(theta) = 0.5.

examples: If u = 50 m/s, theta = 30 degrees, and g = 10 m/s^2, then T = 2(50)(0.5)/10 = 5 s.
raw_text: Projectile motion can be split into horizontal and vertical parts. For launch and landing at the same height, the time of flight is T = 2u sin(theta) / g. With u = 50 m/s, theta = 30 degrees, and g = 10 m/s^2, T = 5 s.
